generated_comment: Yaar, I heard about Sameer and that new girl, Ritu, in his team. His wife, poor thing, must be devastated. But honestly, Ritu knew he was married, right? She was always so extra friendly with him. These girls, they just don't care about breaking homes. Bechara Sameer, he probably got swayed, but she definitely put him in a tough spot.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment is misogynistic as it blames the woman (Ritu) entirely for an affair, accusing her of 'breaking homes' and 'putting him in a tough spot' due to her 'extra friendliness', while excusing the man (Sameer) as 'bechara' who 'got swayed'. This directly encodes the 'Homewrecker Framing' where the woman is seen as a seductress and held solely responsible for male infidelity.
original_frame: Gendered Division of Chores via Practicality
persona_text: You are Rahul. Age 29. Married for one year. A software engineer in Bengaluru. You and your wife Neha both work full time. Neha has been saying for three weeks that the division of household work is not equal Ñ she cooks, she cleans, she manages groceries, she does the laundry. You do the dishes sometimes and take out the trash. You are not a bad person and you do not think of yourself as traditional. But you are on a phone call with your college friend tonight after Neha brought it up again at dinner and you are telling your friend your side of the story and why you think the current arrangement actually makes sense given both your situations.
